title: centaurea alba subsp. sterilis
intro: Centaurea alba subsp. sterilis, also known as White Knapweed, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the family Asteraceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Centaurea alba subsp. sterilis include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate digestive issues, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support digestive health and promote overall well-being.
